---
title: "Quarto and Publishing"
subtitle: "Week 05"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-copy: true
---

## Purpose

Week 05 is the first project communication milestone. You will use Quarto to publish a short slide deck that explains your research question, data, workflow, current results, and next steps.

## By the end of this week

You should be able to:

- Build a Quarto slide deck.
- Present code-derived figures clearly.
- Explain your project endpoint to an interdisciplinary audience.
- Distinguish current results from planned work.
- Use presentation feedback to revise your project plan.

## What a Week 5 presentation should do

Your Week 5 slides should make the project legible, not finished. The audience should understand:

- What question you are asking.
- Why the data are appropriate.
- Which workflow you are using.
- What you have successfully run.
- Where the biggest risk remains.
- What you will complete by Week 10.

## Reproducible presentation practice

Whenever possible, figures should be generated from code and saved in `output/`. The slide deck can then include the figure as a file rather than relying on a manual screenshot.

## Slide structure

A useful structure is:

1. Title and research question.
2. Study system or data source.
3. Workflow diagram.
4. Current result or diagnostic.
5. Main blocker or decision.
6. Four-week plan.

## Project checkpoint

After presenting, update your project issue with:

- Feedback you received.
- One change to your endpoint or workflow.
- The next concrete task.

## References

- Quarto presentations
- Roberts Lab Handbook: Data Management
- Course project-proposal and weekly-progress issue forms
